user.title: OpenAI Tunnel for HA-MCP
user.description: Enable Home Assistant's MCP Server integration, then paste its local/direct MCP URL, normally http://homeassistant.local:8123/api/mcp. Create or inspect your OpenAI tunnel at https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/tunnels. The OpenAI tunnel ID is the tunnel_... value from that page; it identifies the OpenAI-hosted tunnel endpoint that this Home Assistant integration and ChatGPT both attach to. It is not the runtime API key, Home Assistant token, or MCP URL. Create a runtime API key at https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/api-keys with Tunnels Read and Tunnels Use. For private Home Assistant OAuth, use a Home Assistant long-lived access token here and configure the ChatGPT connector as No Auth. After this integration reports ready, add the ChatGPT connector at https://chatgpt.com/plugins#settings/Connectors?create-connector=true&redirectAfter=%2Fplugins.

error.invalid_tunnel_id: Tunnel IDs must look like tunnel_ followed by 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters.
error.invalid_mcp_url: Enter a valid HTTP or HTTPS HA-MCP URL.
error.missing_mcp_path: Enter the full HA-MCP URL path, such as /api/mcp.
error.default_mcp_path: Do not use the generic /mcp path. Use Home Assistant's /api/mcp endpoint or the full path shown by your HA-MCP server.
error.invalid_input: Check the values and try again.

init.title: OpenAI Tunnel for HA-MCP
init.description: Update the OpenAI tunnel ID, runtime API key, HA-MCP URL, optional HA-MCP bearer token, or non-default control-plane endpoint. Inspect tunnels at https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/tunnels and runtime keys at https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/api-keys. Use the same tunnel_... ID in the ChatGPT connector at https://chatgpt.com/plugins#settings/Connectors?create-connector=true&redirectAfter=%2Fplugins.

error.invalid_tunnel_id: Tunnel IDs must look like tunnel_ followed by 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters.
error.invalid_mcp_url: Enter a valid HTTP or HTTPS HA-MCP URL.
error.missing_mcp_path: Enter the full HA-MCP URL path, such as /api/mcp.
error.default_mcp_path: Do not use the generic /mcp path. Use Home Assistant's /api/mcp endpoint or the full path shown by your HA-MCP server.
error.invalid_input: Check the values and try again.

unsupported_arch.title: Unsupported tunnel-client platform
unsupported_arch.description: OpenAI Tunnel for HA-MCP only has pinned tunnel-client v0.0.10 downloads for Linux amd64 and Linux arm64. This host reported {platform}.

startup_failed.title: OpenAI Tunnel for HA-MCP failed to start

binary_download_failed.title: tunnel-client download failed
binary_download_failed.description: The pinned tunnel-client binary could not be downloaded or verified: {error}

mcp_url_unreachable.title: HA-MCP URL is not reachable
mcp_url_unreachable.description: The configured HA-MCP URL could not be reached: {url}

public_mcp_url.title: HA-MCP URL does not look local
public_mcp_url.description: The configured HA-MCP URL is not loopback or private LAN: {url}. Prefer the direct local HA-MCP URL and let OpenAI Tunnel handle remote access.

https_mcp_url.title: HA-MCP URL uses HTTPS
https_mcp_url.description: The configured HA-MCP URL uses HTTPS: {url}. This is allowed, but the recommended setup is a local/direct HA-MCP URL.

invalid_credentials.title: Tunnel credentials were rejected
invalid_credentials.description: Create a runtime API key with Tunnels Read and Tunnels Use, then update the integration options.

readiness_timeout.title: Tunnel readiness timed out
readiness_timeout.description: tunnel-client started but did not publish a healthy readiness URL in time.
